response.justification: The candidate is a strong senior individual contributor with deep, varied engineering experience (15+ years) and genuine developer-tooling credentials (test runners, assertion libraries, CI/CD infrastructure, device orchestration tooling, MCP code generators). However, this is a Level II people-management role requiring at least 2 years of formal people management and 3 years of technical leadership, and the resume shows no evidence of managing people, running one-on-ones, setting team priorities, or leading organizational change. Mobile experience is present but dated and thin. He would be a credible fit for a senior/staff IC role on this team, but not for the manager position as described.
strengths: Extensive hands-on developer-tools background: test frameworks (Trial, Fluent-Asserts), CI/CD setup, test automation, and AI-agent/MCP tooling
15+ years of broad engineering experience across web, mobile, desktop, and high-throughput backend systems
Demonstrated mentorship and knowledge-sharing via workshops, courses, and community talks (Selenium course, coding dojos)
concerns: No formal people-management experience — no direct reports, performance reviews, or team-leadership roles listed
No evidence of strategic roadmap planning or leading organizational change at team/org level
Mobile-platform experience is limited and dated (iOS/Apple TV work ended in 2016)

key_factors.factor: No demonstrated org-level technical leadership or roadmap ownership
